summary: Joel announces the launch of the first Stack Exchange site to complete the community creation process and exit beta. The site, initially called nothingtoinstall.com, focuses on helping users with web application questions. He provides example questions covering common web app tasks like emailing large files, deleting Facebook accounts, and backing up WordPress blogs. The post includes an update noting the site was quickly renamed to webapps.stackexchange.com because the original name was unpopular.
key_points: The first community-created Stack Exchange site successfully graduated from beta to full launch
The site focuses on practical how-to questions about consumer web applications
The original branding (nothingtoinstall.com) was abandoned within days due to community feedback, showing willingness to iterate on naming
money_quotes: The first Stack Exchange site to make it all the way through the community creation process is now live and out of beta!
Only days after launching, we changed the name from "nothingtoinstall.com" to "webapps.stackexchange.com" because nobody really liked that name!
key_insight: The successful launch of the first community-driven Stack Exchange site validated the Area 51 process for growing the Q&A network beyond programming, even if naming decisions needed quick course correction.
